title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

description: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a comforting and nutritious staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. This hearty soup is packed with flavor from aromatic spices, fresh herbs, and a bright squeeze of lemon, making it a perfect meal for any day. Enjoy this wholesome and traditional dish that warms both body and soul.

ingredients.notes: freshly juiced, plus wedges for serving

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Chop the onion and carrot finely, and mince the garlic. This preparation ensures everything is ready for smooth cooking.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and sauté for 5-7 minutes until they are softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the rinsed lentils, ground cumin, and ground turmeric into the pot. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and coat the lentils with the aromatic flavors. This step deepens the overall taste of the soup.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the 6 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Achieve Desired Consistency
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup directly in the pot. Alternatively, carefully transfer about half of the soup to a regular blender, blend until smooth, and then return it to the pot. This creates a creamy texture while still leaving some whole lentils for body.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed. The lemon juice is crucial for the authentic Lebanese flavor, adding a bright tang that balances the earthiness of the lentils.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and a sprinkle of dried mint, if using. Serve immediately with extra lemon wedges on the side and toasted pita bread for dipping.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a pinch of seven-spice blend along with the cumin and turmeric. This traditional Middle Eastern spice mix elevates the aromatic profile.
Do not skip the lemon juice at the end; it is essential for the authentic bright and tangy flavor of Adas. Adding it at the very end preserves its fresh taste.
If you prefer a smoother soup, you can blend the entire batch until completely creamy. For a chunkier texture, blend less or mash with a potato masher.
article:
The aroma of simmering lentils, fragrant garlic, and bright lemon juice instantly transports you to the heart of the Middle East. Among the many comforting dishes in Lebanese kitchens, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas stands out as a true culinary gem. This humble yet incredibly flavorful soup is a staple in homes across the region, cherished for its nourishing qualities, ease of preparation, and deeply satisfying taste. Whether you're seeking a hearty meal on a chilly evening or a light, healthy lunch, this vibrant soup is a perfect choice, embodying the warmth and hospitality of Lebanese cuisine.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas?
Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas, or simply "Adas" in Arabic, is a traditional Middle Eastern soup renowned for its creamy texture, rich flavor, and nutritional benefits. At its core, this soup is a celebration of simple, wholesome ingredients: red lentils, onions, garlic, and fresh lemon juice. Unlike many other lentil soups that might incorporate meat or a complex array of vegetables, authentic Lebanese Adas often keeps things beautifully minimalist, allowing the natural flavors of the lentils and aromatics to shine. It's naturally vegan and gluten-free, making it an excellent choice for a wide range of dietary preferences. Historically, lentils have been a cornerstone of Middle Eastern diets, providing an affordable and protein-rich food source. This particular soup is often enjoyed during colder months, during Ramadan for breaking the fast, or simply as a comforting everyday meal. The secret to its irresistible taste often lies in the generous use of olive oil, the careful sautéing of onions, and the final flourish of freshly squeezed lemon juice, which brightens and balances the earthy lentils.

## Ingredients You Need
Creating a delicious batch of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas requires just a few key ingredients, most of which you likely already have in your pantry:

*   **Red Lentils:** Approximately 1 cup (200g) – split red lentils are preferred as they cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture.
*   **Onion:** 1 large, finely chopped – the foundation of flavor.
*   **Garlic:** 4-6 cloves, minced – adds a pungent depth.
*   **Olive Oil:** 1/4 cup, plus extra for drizzling – essential for sautéing and finishing.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** 6-8 cups – for the soup's base.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 1/4 cup, freshly squeezed, or to taste – crucial for brightness and tang.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Optional Garnishes:** Fresh parsley or cilantro, a pinch of dried mint, a dash of red pepper flakes (Aleppo pepper is traditional).

## Step-by-Step Instructions
Making a comforting bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a straightforward process that yields incredibly rewarding results. Follow these steps for a perfect batch:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup of red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This helps remove any impurities and excess starch. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1/4 cup olive oil over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Be careful not to brown them too much. Add 4-6 minced garlic cloves and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Combine and Simmer:** Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring them with the onions and garlic for about a minute. Pour in 6-8 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer.
4.  **Cook Until Tender:** Allow the soup to simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have broken down, creating a naturally thick consistency. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. If the soup becomes too thick, add more hot water or broth, a little at a time, until you reach your desired consistency.
5.  **Season and Finish:** Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. You might want more lemon juice for extra tang or more salt. For an even creamier texture, you can use an immersion blender to blend a portion or all of the soup directly in the pot, or carefully transfer batches to a regular blender (blending hot liquids requires caution).
6.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas into bowls. Garnish with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, fresh chopped parsley or cilantro, and a sprinkle of dried mint or red pepper flakes if desired.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results
Achieving that authentic, soul-satisfying taste in your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is all about paying attention to a few details.

*   **Don't Skimp on Olive Oil:** Good quality extra virgin olive oil is not just for cooking; it's a crucial flavor component. A generous drizzle over the finished soup adds richness and a beautiful sheen.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice is Key:** Bottled lemon juice simply won't cut it. The bright, zesty flavor of freshly squeezed lemon is essential for balancing the earthiness of the lentils and giving the soup its characteristic tang. Add it at the very end to preserve its vibrant flavor.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Red lentils break down completely, making the soup naturally thick. If you prefer a thinner soup, simply add more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. If you like it extra creamy, blend a portion of the soup with an immersion blender.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Seasoning is personal. Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Don't be afraid to add a little more of what you love.
*   **Aromatics First:** Properly sautéing the onions until translucent and fragrant, and then briefly cooking the garlic, builds a strong flavor base for the entire soup. Avoid browning the garlic, as it can turn bitter.

## Serving Suggestions
Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a wonderfully versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. It's often served as a comforting main course, especially during cooler weather or as a light, nourishing meal.

*   **With Pita Bread:** The quintessential accompaniment! Warm pita bread, torn into pieces, is perfect for scooping up the creamy soup. You can also toast the pita for a crunchy element.
*   **Fresh Herbs and Olive Oil:** A final flourish of freshly chopped parsley or cilantro, alongside a generous drizzle of good quality extra virgin olive oil, enhances both the flavor and visual appeal. A sprinkle of dried mint is also a classic addition.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Always serve with extra lemon wedges on the side, allowing each diner to add more tang to their liking.
*   **Side Salad:** Pair it with a simple, crisp green salad dressed with a lemon-tahini dressing or a classic Lebanese Fattoush salad for a complete and balanced meal.
*   **Pickles:** A small dish of assorted Middle Eastern pickles (like pickled turnips or cucumbers) provides a delightful contrast to the soup's richness.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes:** For those who enjoy a bit of heat, a pinch of Aleppo pepper or standard red pepper flakes can be sprinkled on top.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is perfect in its simplicity, there are many ways to adapt it to your taste or what you have on hand.

*   **Add Vegetables:** For a heartier soup, you can add finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the lentils. Cook them until tender. Spinach or Swiss chard can also be stirred in during the last few minutes of cooking until wilted.
*   **Spice It Up:** A pinch of ground cumin added with the lentils can enhance the earthy flavor. Some recipes also include a touch of turmeric for a warmer color and subtle flavor, along with its anti-inflammatory benefits.
*   **Tomato Paste:** A tablespoon of tomato paste, sautéed with the onions and garlic, can add a deeper umami note and a richer color to the soup.
*   **Garnish Variations:** Besides parsley and mint, consider a sprinkle of sumac for an extra lemony tang, or even a swirl of plain yogurt (if not strictly vegan) for creaminess.
*   **Broth Choice:** While water is traditional, using vegetable broth will certainly add more depth of flavor. Chicken broth can also be used if you don't need the soup to be vegetarian/vegan.
*   **Legume Swap:** While red lentils are ideal for their quick cooking time and creamy texture, you could experiment with yellow split peas, though they might require a longer cooking time and a different amount of liquid.

From its humble origins to its widespread appeal, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is more than just a dish; it's a testament to the power of simple, wholesome ingredients transformed into something truly extraordinary. Its comforting warmth, vibrant flavors, and nourishing qualities make it a beloved staple in Middle Eastern cuisine and a fantastic addition to any home cook's repertoire. Whether you're seeking a quick weeknight meal, a healthy lunch, or a taste of authentic Lebanese tradition, this easy-to-make soup promises to deliver satisfaction in every spoonful. Embrace the simplicity, savor the flavors, and enjoy the timeless appeal of this cherished soup.
